summary: WIP attempt to decompile and match the interface/status HUD module `ifstatus`. The PR added new `src/melee/if/ifstatus.c` and `ifstatus.h` files with a mix of handwritten C, inline assembly stubs, placeholder structs, globals, externs, and function declarations. The author described it as about 15/26 functions done, with several functions at 99%+ needing small tweaks. The PR was closed for inactivity, but a later comment says its changes were merged into master via PR #494.

key_files.role: new implementation file for the ifstatus decompilation attempt; contains globals, externs, C functions, and many inline assembly functions
new header with HUD-related structs, placeholder return types, extern declarations, and ifstatus function prototypes

what_changed: Added `src/melee/if/ifstatus.c` as a new file for the `ifstatus` module.
Added `src/melee/if/ifstatus.h` with include guards, HSD/player includes, typedefs, externs, and function declarations.
Introduced global HUD state symbols including `s8 lbl_804D6D60` and `HudIndex lbl_804A10C8`, described in-code as a global array of HUD info.

Defined HUD-oriented structures including `HudFlags`, `HudValue`, `HudIndex`, `Element_803F9628`, `Thing_803F9628`, `Coordinate3D`, and placeholder structs for unknown return values from `func_8016AE38` and `func_8016AE50`.

Modeled HUD digit state with explicit fields for damage percent, old damage, last-attack damage, shake frames, per-digit x/y translations, per-digit velocities, and `HSD_JObj*` pointers for hundreds/tens/ones/percent-sign jobjs.
Added bitfield-style HUD flags such as `explode_animation`, `force_digit_shake`, `hide_all_digits`, and `animation_status_id`.
Added many `asm void` functions where matching C was not yet completed, including early functions such as `func_802F491C`, `func_802F4B84`, and larger later functions.

Added several C functions around player HUD lifecycle and status behavior, including `func_802F4910`, `func_802F6194`, `func_802F665C`, `func_802F6788`, `func_802F6804`, `func_802F6898`, `func_802F68F0`, `func_802F6948`, `func_802F69C0`, `func_802F6AF8`, `func_802F6C04`, `func_802F6D10`, `func_802F6E1C`, `func_802F6E3C`, and `func_802F7220`.

Implemented simple HUD cleanup helpers that destroy `HUD_parent_entity` and `next` GObjs via `func_80390228` or hide/suspend them via `func_80390CAC`, then call cleanup hooks such as `func_802FB650` or `func_802FB6AC`.
Added functions to hide and show all percentage digits by toggling `players[i].flags.hide_all_digits` for six HUD player slots.

Included comments with decomp.me scratch links and near-match percentages, for example `99.81% match` for `func_802F6194`, `99.94% complete` for `func_802F69C0`, and `99.93% complete` for several related player-status functions.
The PR was not merged directly; it was closed due to inactivity, and a later maintainer comment states the changes were merged into master via #494.
smart_moves: Separated the reconstruction into a source file and a header, making the recovered HUD structs and function prototypes reusable by later `ifstatus` work.
Used descriptive struct and field names where evidence was clear enough, especially for HUD damage digits, digit translations, digit velocities, `hide_all_digits`, and `explode_animation`.

Kept uncertain areas explicit with placeholder names like `Placeholder_8016AE38_ret_val`, `Placeholder_8016AE50_ret_val`, `UnknownClassTypeE`, and `unk*` fields instead of pretending the types were fully understood.
Preserved progress on hard functions with inline assembly while still landing C versions for simpler or nearly matched routines.
Recorded decomp.me scratch URLs and near-match percentages directly in comments, making it easier for future contributors to resume the exact matching work.
Captured repeated HUD-player operations as concrete C patterns: index into `lbl_804A10C8.players`, update flags, destroy or hide GObjs, and call matching downstream cleanup/status callbacks.
Reconstructed packed flags as C bitfields, which exposed semantic flag usage while still preserving unknown bits.
Documented offset assumptions in the header comments, which helps future struct-size and layout verification.
decomp_lessons: For large HUD/interface files, it is useful to split progress between matched C, nearly matched C with decomp.me links, and inline assembly placeholders rather than blocking all progress on the hardest functions.
A central module-global struct such as `HudIndex` can unlock many functions once repeated field offsets are named and shared through a header.
When a field is only partially understood, prefer a descriptive-but-honest name plus an offset comment over an overconfident final name.
Repeated code patterns around `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub`, JObj transforms, and GObj cleanup can identify struct fields such as digit JObjs, parent GObjs, and per-digit translation/velocity state.
Bitfield reconstruction is valuable for HUD flags, but the exact bit ordering and semantic names should be checked carefully against emitted `rlwinm`/`rlwimi` code.
Near-matches should record the exact scratch link and percentage so future work can reproduce the remaining mismatch rather than rediscover it.

If adding extern data just to satisfy a function, mark uncertainty clearly; this PR includes a comment around `extern Thing_803F9628 lbl_803F9628` saying it was probably garbage, which is a useful caution for later cleanup.
Closed WIP PRs can still contain useful decomp research; here, maintainers later noted that changes from this PR were merged via #494.
naming_conventions: Unknown functions retained address-based names such as `func_802F4910`, `func_802F6194`, and `func_802F7220`.
Unknown data symbols retained label-based names such as `lbl_804D6D60`, `lbl_804A10C8`, and `lbl_803F9628`.
Recovered HUD-facing types used CamelCase typedefs with underscore-prefixed struct tags, for example `typedef struct _HudValue { ... } HudValue;`.
Partially understood fields used `unk*` names, often with offset comments, rather than speculative semantic names.
Placeholder types were explicitly named after the producing function, e.g. `Placeholder_8016AE38_ret_val` and `Placeholder_8016AE50_ret_val`.

Some semantic names were introduced for confident HUD concepts: `damage_percent`, `old_damage`, `frames_of_shake_remaining`, `hide_all_digits`, `hundreds_digit_x_translation`, `percent_sign_y_velocity`, and related digit JObj pointers.
Header guard style used `_ifstatus_h_`, matching the new header name but not a modern all-caps convention.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used inline `asm void` definitions for functions that were not yet matched or were too large to reverse immediately.
Mixed C functions with inline assembly in a single module, allowing partial matching progress without requiring complete source reconstruction.
Added comments such as `99.81% match`, `99.94% complete`, `99.93% complete`, and `in progress` with decomp.me scratch links to preserve matching state.
Used an inline helper `nth_node` to express traversal logic for `func_802F6194`, which was documented as a 99.81% match.
Represented byte flags with bitfields in `HudFlags` and placeholder flag structs to make `rlwinm`/`rlwimi` behavior more tractable in C.

Kept many HSD operations visible in C/asm context, including `HSD_Randf`, `HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub`, `HSD_JObjLoadJoint`, `HSD_TObjReqAnimAll`, `HSD_TObjAnim`, `GObj_SetupGXLink`, and GObj destroy/hide calls.
Relied on exact loop bounds seen in the code: six HUD player slots for `lbl_804A10C8.players` and eight entries for `lbl_803F9628.things`.

follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #494 to see the final merged form of the `ifstatus` work and which names/structs survived review.
Compare current `src/melee/if/ifstatus.c` and `ifstatus.h` against this PR to identify renamed symbols and resolved placeholders.
Search for later renames of `HudIndex`, `HudValue`, `HudFlags`, and `Thing_803F9628`.
Verify whether `lbl_804A10C8.players` still has six entries and whether `lbl_803F9628.things` still has eight entries in current master.

Look for later matches of the inline assembly functions from this PR, especially `func_802F491C`, `func_802F4B84`, `func_802F4EDC`, `func_802F5B48`, `func_802F5EC0`, `func_802F61FC`, `func_802F6508`, `func_802F66A4`, `func_802F6EA4`, `func_802F7034`, and `func_802F7134`.
Check whether the decomp.me scratch links from the comments correspond to final matched functions or stale WIP attempts.
